Tool_leave_encashment_Description: Encash unused leave and estimate taxable vs exempt amount for govt and non-govt employees.
Tool_leave_encashment_Subtitle: Leave payout and tax exemption estimate
Tool_leave_encashment_LearnText: Leave encashment is payment for unused earned leave. Government employees are generally fully exempt. For others, exemption is limited — this tool uses a practical remaining-lifetime-exemption input (default ₹10 lakh) so you can see how much of the payout may be taxable.
Tool_leave_encashment_FormulaNote: Common payroll formula uses (Basic + DA) ÷ 30 × leave days. Statutory Sec 10(10AA) also caps by 10 months’ salary and earned-leave balance — we simplify to an editable exemption remaining. Confirm with your CA / Form 16.
Tool_leave_encashment_WorkingDaysHint: Most employers use 30. Some use working days in the month.
Tool_leave_encashment_DailyRateHint: If your payslip already shows a daily leave rate, enter it here.
Tool_leave_encashment_ExemptionHint: Lifetime leave-encashment exemption still available. Default ₹10 lakh — lower it if you used exemption earlier.
Tool_leave_encashment_ScenarioSubtitle: Same salary rate, different leave days
Tool_leave_encashment_ExampleStep_4: Exemption remaining ₹10 lakh → entire ₹50,000 is exempt.
Tool_leave_encashment_ExampleStep_5: If amount exceeded remaining exemption, the excess would be taxable.
Tool_leave_encashment_Step_1: Derive daily Basic+DA rate (÷30 or enter daily).
Tool_leave_encashment_Step_3: Apply government full exemption or remaining cap.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_Purpose: Estimate leave encashment amount and how much may be taxable.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_WhyKnow: Retirement or resignation leave payouts can create a surprise tax bill if exemption is exhausted.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_RealLife: Resignation settlement, retirement, or mid-career leave buyout.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_Alt_Salary_Wh: For full CTC → in-hand, not leave payout.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_Alt_Gratuity_: For gratuity on exit, not leave cash.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To see how taxable leave adds to annual tax.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_Differentiati: FinYatra shows amount and a practical exemption-remaining tax split in one place.
Tool_leave_encashment_Info_Motto: Know the payout — and the taxable slice.
Tool_da_Description: Dearness Allowance from basic pay and DA%. See DA amount and Basic+DA total.
Tool_da_LearnText: Dearness Allowance (DA) is a cost-of-living allowance linked to inflation, paid as a percentage of basic pay for many government and PSU employees. Private employers may also use a DA component in salary structure.
Tool_da_FormulaNote: DA% is notified periodically (e.g. central government DA revisions). Enter the % that applies to your pay scale.
Tool_da_Result_TotalFootnote: Often used for HRA / leave / gratuity bases
Tool_da_ExampleStep_5: A 10-point DA hike would add ₹5,000 more DA.
Tool_da_Info_Purpose: Calculate DA amount and Basic+DA from a DA percentage.
Tool_da_Info_WhyKnow: DA revisions change take-home and many salary-linked benefits.
Tool_da_Info_RealLife: Central/state pay revisions, PSU salary, comparing offers with DA.
Tool_da_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra isolates DA% math and side-by-side rate scenarios.
Tool_advance_tax_Description: Quarterly advance tax schedule: 15 Jun 15%, 15 Sep 45%, 15 Dec 75%, 15 Mar 100%.
Tool_advance_tax_Subtitle: Stay on track with quarterly installments
Tool_advance_tax_LearnText: If your estimated annual tax (after TDS) exceeds the threshold, you generally pay advance tax in installments. This tool shows how much should be paid by each due date and whether you have a shortfall versus tax already paid.
Tool_advance_tax_FormulaNote: Schedule shown is the common individual / non-presumptive pattern. Presumptive taxpayers and companies can differ. Interest under 234B/234C is not computed here.
Tool_advance_tax_EstimatedHint: Total income-tax you expect for the year (before considering this schedule).
Tool_advance_tax_PaidHint: Include TDS deducted and advance tax already deposited this FY.
Tool_advance_tax_LabelUseDate: Pick installment from current / as-of date
Tool_advance_tax_Result_Required: Should have paid by due date
Tool_advance_tax_Result_ShortfallFootnot: Pay at least this much now
Tool_advance_tax_SuggestedNote: Suggested payment to clear shortfall by {1}: {0}
Tool_advance_tax_ScenarioSubtitle: Same estimate, different tax already paid
Tool_advance_tax_ExampleTitle: Worked example: ₹2 lakh tax, ₹20,000 paid by Sep
Tool_advance_tax_ExampleStep_2: By 15 Sep you should have paid 45% = ₹90,000.
Tool_advance_tax_Step_4: Compare required cumulative vs paid for shortfall.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Purpose: Plan quarterly advance tax against estimated liability and tax already paid.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_WhyKnow: Missing installment percentages can attract interest; a clear schedule helps cash-flow planning.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_RealLife: Self-employed, freelancers, capital gains years, high non-salary income.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Goal_1: See remaining tax for the year
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Goal_3: Spot shortfall vs payments so far
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Alt_Tax_When: To estimate the annual liability first.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Alt_Salary_When: Salaried with mostly TDS — advance tax may not apply.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Differentiation: FinYatra maps date or installment to cumulative % and shortfall in one view.
Tool_advance_tax_Info_Motto: Pay on time — avoid interest surprises.
